extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI Michał attacked on the penultimate gravel sector with 2km to sector's end, stealing meters via 'stealth attack' while others watched each other
tactical_patterns: Kwiatkowski's winning move came on gravel sector exit, not ascent—exploiting transitional moment when rivals anticipated tarmac recovery.
Teams prioritized domestique pacing on gravel sectors to deliver leaders, allowing stealthier solo accelerations to escape uncontested.
Group of seven fragmented not on steepest gravel but final 2km approaching tarmac, revealing fatigue timing vulnerability.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: positive: Second Strade win confirms ability to execute solo gravel accelerations when rivals race reactively rather than proactively
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: Second place again shows inability to respond to gravel accelerations despite positioning—always chasing never initiating
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: First podium demonstrates improving durability on longer gravel sectors, breaking from pure sprint specialist limitations
rider_signals.STYBAR Zdeněk: negative: Fourth place despite Quickstep numbers suggests over-reliance on teammates' pace rather than personal gravel acceleration capacity
